article_title: AMD, Arm, and Qualcomm Invest $60 Million in Wayve, Giving the Self-Driving Startup Backing From Every Major Automotive Chip Vendor

content_preview.title: AMD, Arm, and Qualcomm Invest $60 Million in Wayve, Giving the Self-Driving Startup Backing From Every Major Automotive Chip Vendor
content_preview.summary: Wayve's Series D extension brings total funding to $1.26 billion as the UK startup secures investment from four chipmakers whose silicon underpins virtually every automotive compute platform.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Chipmakers AMD, Arm, and Qualcomm Ventures have invested $60 million in UK autonomous driving startup Wayve, extending its Series D round to $1.26 billion and giving the company investment relationships with all four major providers of automotive compute silicon. The extension, announced April 15, follows NVIDIA's participation in the original $1.2 billion close in February 2026, as reported by [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/15/chipmakers-amd-arm-and-qualcomm-are-all-in...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong AV business article on Wayve's chip vendor investment alignment.
editor_notes.source_verification: Sources captured (TechCrunch, The Next Web). $60M Series D extension, AMD/Arm/Qualcomm Ventures, all 4 major chip vendors now backing, Nissan/Uber/Mercedes/Stellantis partnerships from captured snapshots.
editor_notes.originality: Updates prior Wayve $1.5B coverage with chip vendor angle.
